ocr.text: In a comparative analysis of health metrics between men and women based on physical activity levels categorized by METs, the
data shows that for Non-HDL-C, mg/dL, men with <10 METs (n = 51) had a mean of 164 ± 35, whereas those with ≥10 METs
(n = 221) had 165 ± 36 with a p value of NS; women exhibited 160 ± 39 for <10 METs (n = 99) and 156 ± 39 for ≥10 METs (n
= 155), also with a p value of NS. For HDL-C, mg/dL, men recorded 48 ± 13 for <10 METs and 49 ± 11 for ≥10 METs, both
with NS p values, while women had 62 ± 16 for <10 METs and 65 ± 17 for ≥10 METs, likewise NS. Regarding SBP, mmHg,
men showed 134 ± 14 for <10 METs and 126 ± 12 for ≥10 METs, with a significant p value of <0.001; women had 123 ± 12 for
<10 METs and 118 ± 12 for ≥10 METs, also <0.001. DBP, mmHg results indicated 83 ± 10 for men with <10 METs and 78 ± 7
for ≥10 METs, p < 0.01, while women showed 76 ± 6 and 73 ± 7 respectively, also p < 0.01. Blood glucose, mg/dL readings
were 104 ± 14 for men (<10 METs) and 94 ± 10 (≥10 METs), p < 0.01, with women at 89 ± 8 and 88 ± 9, p value NS.
Variable  Men  Women
<10 METs(n = 51)  ≥10 METs(n = 221)  p value  <10 METs(n = 99)  ≥10 METs(n = 155)  p value
Blood glucose, mg/dL  104 ± 14  94 ± 10  <0.01  89 ± 8  88 ± 9  NS
Waist circumference, cm measurements revealed 102 ± 10 for men (<10 METs) and 97 ± 8 (≥10 METs), p < 0.001, while
women showed 88 ± 11 and 80 ± 9, p < 0.0001. The BMI, kg/m2 for men was 29 ± 4 for <10 METs and 27 ± 3 for ≥10 METs, p
< 0.01, whereas women had 28 ± 4 and 25 ± 3, p < 0.0001. The ACC/AHA score, % for men was 13 ± 6 (<10 METs) and 10 ± 4
(≥10 METs), p < 0.01; women showed 4 ± 2 and 3.5 ± 2 respectively, p value of 0.06. The prevalence of ≥2 risk factors, % was
84% for men with <10 METs and 69% for those with ≥10 METs, p value 0.04, while women reported 61% for <10 METs and
42% for ≥10 METs, with a significant p value of <0.01.
